title: Gradient-Based Learning Applied to Document Recognition
authors: Yann LeCun, Léon Bottou, Yoshua Bengio, Patrick Haffner
content:
# Summary

This paper introduces the MNIST handwritten digit dataset and demonstrates how gradient-based learning with convolutional neural networks can effectively solve image classification tasks. It established a standard benchmark for evaluating classification algorithms.

# Key Insights

- Convolutional Neural Networks can automatically learn hierarchical features from raw images.
- Gradient-based optimization enables end-to-end training without manual feature engineering.
- MNIST provides

title: Why millennials and gen Z are silently leaving their jobs? Unraveling the 'quiet quitting' trend
authors: Nuryanti Taufik, Agi Rosyadi, Muhammad Aliyuddin
method: Survey + Structural Equation Modelling (SEM) with AMOS
content:
# Summary

This paper investigates the drivers of "quiet quitting" — working strictly within assigned tasks with no extra effort or emotional investment — among millennial and Gen Z workers in Indonesia. Using self-determination theory as a lens and SEM on 600 survey responses, the authors test four hypotheses linking empowering leadership, organizational culture, and work overload to job satisfaction, and job satisfaction to quiet quitting.
